title: campanula pusilla subsp. excisa
intro: Campanula pusilla subsp. excisa, also known as the dwarf bellflower or alpine bellflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
